alignments.rationale: Both texts present the same result claim with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adapted convolutional-recurrent neural network for detecting and classifying cardiac arrhythmias achieves a drop in $F_1$ score from 0.8 to 0.784 when implemented in fixed-point precision on the nRF52 microcontroller.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adapted convolutional-recurrent neural network for detecting and classifying cardiac arrhythmias achieves a drop in F1 score from 0.8 to 0.784 when implemented in fixed-point precision.
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and context (fixed-point implementation) are the same; the publication omits explicit mention of the nRF52 microcontroller but the substantive performance claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The memory footprint of the neural network implementation on the nRF52 microcontroller is 195.6KB.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of the neural network on the nRF52 microcontroller has a memory footprint of 195.6 KB.
alignments.rationale: The memory-footprint claim is identical in value and context between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The throughput of the neural network implementation is 33.98MOps/s.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The throughput of the neural network implementation is 33.98 MOps/s.
alignments.rationale: Throughput value and statement are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoDNNchip can automatically generate both FPGA- and ASIC-based DNN chip implementations for designated applications and datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoDNNchip can automatically produce both FPGA- and ASIC-based DNN chip implementations from DNNs developed by machine learning frameworks without humans in the loop.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specificity (from ML-framework DNNs and "without humans in the loop"), making the claim more specific rather than fundamentally different.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Chip Predictor can accurately and efficiently predict a DNN accelerator's energy, throughput, and area based on various parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Chip Predictor can accurately and efficiently predict a DNN accelerator's energy, throughput, latency, and area based on various parameters and constraints.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds latency and mentions constraints, expanding the set of predicted metrics and clarifying applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted performance of the Chip Predictor differs from real-measured ones by less than 10% when validated using 15 DNN models and 4 platforms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predicted performance of the Chip Predictor differs from real-measured ones by less than 10% when validated using 15 DNN models and 4 platforms.
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is carried over verbatim, with the same datasets and platforms cited.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accelerators generated by AutoDNNchip can achieve up to 3.86X better performance than expert-crafted state-of-the-art accelerators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNN accelerators generated by AutoDNNchip can achieve up to 3.86X better performance than expert-crafted state-of-the-art FPGA- and ASIC-based accelerators.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the comparison is against FPGA- and ASIC-based accelerators, adding specificity to the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bayesian optimization is capable of finding better control solutions with regards to finite and noisy data compared to existing methods of optimal control.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian optimization is capable of finding better control solutions with regards to finite and noisy data compared to existing methods of optimal control.
alignments.rationale: The core result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The probabilistic modeling and broad exploration aspects of Bayesian optimization are particularly suitable for quantum experiments where data acquisition can be expensive.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The probabilistic modeling and broad exploration aspects of Bayesian optimization are particularly suitable for quantum experiments where data acquisition can be expensive.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory conclusion is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study explores a quantum optimal control framework based on Bayesian optimization to prepare desired quantum states despite decoherence and system imperfections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum optimal control based on Bayesian optimization can help pave the way to a specific target state despite decoherence and system imperfections.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the exploration into a more actionable claim that BO-based control can help reach target states, slightly strengthening the practical implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement Learning algorithms can be effectively tailored for long-term planning in microgrid energy storage systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement Learning algorithms can be effectively tailored for long-term planning in microgrid energy storage systems.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion is unchanged in wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model and expansion plan have been developed to optimally determine microgrid designs that can dynamically react to changing conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model and expansion plan have been developed to optimally determine microgrid designs that can dynamically react to changing conditions.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal storage capacity threshold for a microgrid system is significantly influenced by the price movements of available storage units.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal storage capacity threshold for a microgrid system is significantly influenced by the price movements of available storage units.
alignments.rationale: The reported result is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approaches allow for modeling inherent problem uncertainties and optimizing sequential investment decision-making in energy storage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approaches allow for modeling inherent problem uncertainties and optimizing sequential investment decision-making in energy storage.
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same, but the publication assigns a lower certainty level (medium) than the preprint (high), indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed technique using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a focal loss function effectively addresses the class imbalance problem in the classification of dog red blood cell morphology. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed technique using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) with a focal loss function effectively addresses the class imbalance problem in the classification of dog red blood cell morphology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are textually identical in meaning; only the claim type label differs, so the claim content and strength are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both CNN models augmented with the focal loss function achieved higher F1-scores compared to models using a conventional cross-entropy loss function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both CNN models augmented with the focal loss function achieved higher F1-scores compared to models using a conventional cross-entropy loss function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is identical between preprint and publication with no change in wording or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The focal loss function enables CNN models to be less biased towards the majority class in the classification task of imbalanced dog red blood cell data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The focal loss function enables CNN models to be less biased towards the majority class in the classification task of imbalanced dog red blood cell data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over verbatim from the preprint to the publication, with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed mixed integer linear programming (MILP) model offers a rigorous yet efficient method for grid generation that ensures packing, alignment, grouping, and preferential positioning of elements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mixed integer linear programming (MILP) model offers a rigorous yet efficient method for grid generation that ensures packing, alignment, grouping, and preferential positioning of elements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical in wording and confidence between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The techniques for interactive diversification, enhancement, and completion of grid layouts are effective in improving the design process. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Techniques for interactive diversification, enhancement, and completion of grid layouts are presented. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the preprint's effectiveness claim to a presentation of techniques, reducing assertiveness; scope remains the same but claim strength is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings from a ratings study and a design study provide evidence for the benefit of computational grid generation during early stages of design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings from a ratings study and a design study provide evidence for the benefit of computational grid generation during early stages of design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The results claim is the same in both versions, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ViPeD, a new synthetically generated set of images, allows for automatic annotation and is essential for training pedestrian detection models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ViPeD, a new synthetically generated set of images, allows for automatic annotation and is essential for training pedestrian detection models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training solely on the synthetic dataset leads to a Synthetic2Real Domain Shift, resulting in a performance drop when applied to real-world images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training solely on the synthetic dataset leads to a Synthetic2Real domain shift, resulting in a performance drop when applied to real-world images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about domain shift is phrased equivalently in both versions with the same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Domain Adaptation techniques can reduce the Synthetic2Real Domain Shift and improve performance on real-world images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed domain adaptation techniques reduce the Synthetic2Real domain shift and improve performance on real-world images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same; the publication frames it as a result rather than a conclusion, but the substantive claim and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network trained with ViPeD generalizes over unseen real-world scenarios better than the detector trained solely on real-world data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network trained with ViPeD generalizes over unseen real-world scenarios better than the detector trained solely on real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high), so the substantive claim is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel dynamic coronary roadmapping approach improves visual feedback and reduces contrast use during PCI. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel dynamic coronary roadmapping approach improves visual feedback and reduces contrast use during PCI. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method compensates for cardiac and respiratory induced vessel motion by using ECG alignment and catheter tip tracking. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method compensates for cardiac and respiratory induced vessel motion by using ECG alignment and catheter tip tracking. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried forward verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning based Bayesian filtering method proposed for catheter tip tracking integrates detection outcomes from a convolutional neural network and motion estimation using a particle filtering framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new deep learning based Bayesian filtering method integrates detection outcomes from a convolutional neural network and motion estimation using a particle filtering framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The descriptions are equivalent (minor adjective ordering), so the claim content and confidence remain unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The roadmapping and tracking approaches were validated on clinical X-ray images, achieving accurate performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed roadmapping and tracking approaches were validated on clinical X-ray images, achieving accurate performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Validation result is the same in both versions with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach runs in real-time on a computer with a single GPU and can be integrated into the clinical workflow of PCI procedures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach runs in real-time on a computer with a single GPU and can be integrated into the clinical workflow of PCI procedures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The implementation/conclusion claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inverse binomial sampling (IBS) can estimate the log-likelihood of an entire data set efficiently and without bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inverse binomial sampling (IBS) can estimate the log-likelihood of an entire data set efficiently and without bias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical in text and confidence between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variance of the IBS estimator is uniformly bounded and achieves the minimum variance for an unbiased estimator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The variance of the IBS estimator is uniformly bounded and achieves the minimum variance for an unbiased estimator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The estimator variance claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IBS generally produces lower error in the estimated parameters and maximum log-likelihood values than alternative sampling methods with the same average number of samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: IBS generally produces lower error in the estimated parameters and maximum log-likelihood values than alternative sampling methods with the same average number of samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the potential of IBS as a practical, robust, and easy to implement method for log-likelihood evaluation when exact techniques are not available. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate the potential of IBS as a practical, robust, and easy to implement method for log-likelihood evaluation when exact techniques are not available. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding assessment of IBS is the same in both versions with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed composite framework, called Blended inverse-PDE networks (BiPDE networks), effectively finds unknown fields in inverse problems for partial differential equations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed composite framework effectively finds unknown fields in inverse problems for partial differential equations.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution (BiPDE framework finding unknown fields) with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BiPDE networks blend deep neural networks with existing numerical algorithms for partial differential equations to enhance data efficiency in discovering hidden fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network incorporates domain-specific knowledge and physical constraints through a hard-coded PDE solver layer, enhancing data efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the data-efficiency claim but clarifies the mechanism (domain knowledge and hard-coded PDE solver) compared with the preprint's higher-level description of 'blending'.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture incorporates a hard-coded PDE solver layer, making it explicitly aware of governing physics, unlike most existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network incorporates domain-specific knowledge and physical constraints through a hard-coded PDE solver layer, enhancing data efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the hard-coded PDE-solver claim but frames it in terms of incorporating domain knowledge and improving data efficiency rather than explicit contrast to most existing methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BiPDE networks demonstrate applicability in recovering variable diffusion coefficients in Poisson problems and the nonlinear Burgers' equation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture, called Blended inverse-PDE networks (BiPDE networks), demonstrates applicability for recovering variable diffusion coefficients in Poisson problems and the Burgers' equation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same application examples (Poisson and Burgers') and the applicability claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is robust to noise, indicating its reliability in practical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned hidden parameters in the BiPDE networks are robust to added noise on input data.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert robustness to added/input noise; the publication restates this result with similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There will always exist unpredicted events and conditions that autonomous systems will encounter.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There will always exist unpredicted events and conditions that autonomous systems must handle.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about inevitability of unpredicted events with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Handling unpredicted events and conditions in autonomous systems is possible despite their unpredictability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Handling unpredicted situations in autonomous systems is possible despite their unpredictability.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about the possibility of handling unpredicted situations with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Design principles can be applied in advance to enable systems to deal with unpredicted circumstances in the future.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Design principles can improve an autonomous system's ability to deal with unpredicted situations when applied in advance.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that applying design principles in advance improves handling of unpredicted situations; wording is slightly adjusted but meaning and confidence are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work contributes toward a foundation of engineering principles for developing trustworthy next-generation autonomous systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work contributes toward a foundation of engineering principles for developing trustworthy next-generation autonomous systems.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim retained in the publication with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AidMe is an adaptive intent detection framework that allows digital assistants to learn user intents as interactions progress.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AidMe is an adaptive intent detection framework that allows digital assistants to learn user intents as interactions progress.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the exact description of AidMe's core adaptive intent-detection contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AidMe builds its repertoire of intents and collects data to train a model of semantic similarity evaluation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AidMe builds a repertoire of intents and collects data to train a model of semantic similarity evaluation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this mechanism of building intents and collecting data for training with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AidMe addresses the issues of intent learning and user adaptation for instructable digital assistants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AidMe addresses the issues of intent learning and user adaptation for instructable digital assistants.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claimed scope of AidMe addressing intent learning and user adaptation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intent identification system in traditional assistants should be improved to recognize new forms of known intents and acquire new intents expressed by users.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intent identification system in traditional assistants needs to improve by recognizing new forms of known intents and acquiring new intents expressed by users.
alignments.rationale: Substantively the same limitation/recommendation is present in the publication though labeled as a 'limitation' rather than a 'conclusion' in the metadata.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Natural Language Understanding module should be sample efficient and continuously collect training data without relying on a pretrained model.
alignments.rationale: This claim about sample-efficient NLU and avoiding pretrained models does not appear in the preprint claims and is newly stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We introduce a deep neural network based method for solving a class of elliptic partial differential equations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a deep neural network based method for solving a class of elliptic partial differential equations.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution of a neural-network method for elliptic PDEs is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The solution is given by an expectation of a martingale process driven by a Brownian motion.
alignments.rationale: The specific analytic characterization via an expectation of a martingale driven by Brownian motion is not reflected in the publication's listed claims, indicating it was omitted from the final claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is a 'Derivative-Free Loss Method' since it does not require the explicit calculation of the derivatives of the neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method is a ‘Derivative-Free Loss Method’ since it does not require the explicit calculation of the derivatives of the neural network with respect to the input neurons in order to compute the training loss.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the derivative-free claim but provides a more precise formulation about which derivatives are not required for loss computation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advantages of our method are showcased in a series of test problems including a corner singularity problem and an interface problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advantages of our method are showcased in a series of test problems including a corner singularity problem, a high-dimensional Poisson's equation, an interface problem, and an application to a chemotaxis population model.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the list of benchmark problems and applications, broadening the demonstrated advantages while preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new framework for Adam-type methods incorporates trend information, which enhances parameter updates with adaptive step size and gradients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new framework for Adam-type methods incorporates trend information, which enhances parameter updates with adaptive step size and gradients.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same description of the framework and its use of trend information.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The additional terms in the proposed algorithm lead to more efficient movement on the complex cost surface, resulting in faster convergence of the loss.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The additional terms in the proposed algorithm lead to more efficient movement on the complex cost surface, resulting in faster convergence of the loss.
alignments.rationale: The claim about improved movement on the cost surface and faster loss convergence is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently outperforms conventional Adam and AMSGrad methods on classical models with several real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical results demonstrate that the new framework consistently outperforms conventional Adam and AMSGrad methods on classical models with several real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: The empirical outperforming claim is preserved verbatim in the publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-step-training deep learning (TST-DL) framework does not rely on an accurate representation of the imaging model since the model is directly learned.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TST-DL is more robust to model mismatch than approaches which incorporate imperfect knowledge of the imaging model.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the idea as improved robustness to model mismatch (a clearer, empirical statement) rather than the absolute 'does not rely' wording, so the claim is clarified and slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-time imaging can be achieved using the TST-DL framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not mention or support real-time imaging; this capability is not present in the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TST-DL network improves predictions by constraining the first step to learn the model and the second step to learn the optimal regularizer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TST-DL yields better results than end-to-end training while suffering from less overfitting.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the empirical benefit (better results, less overfitting) that follows from the two-step constraint described in the preprint, clarifying the outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TST-DL approach accommodates any size and dimensionality of data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TST-DL framework is a flexible approach for image reconstruction without physics priors, applicable to diverse computational imaging systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication softens the preprint's absolute 'any size/dimensionality' claim to a more modest statement of flexibility and applicability to diverse systems, narrowing and tempering the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework was demonstrated using a linear single-pixel camera imaging model and results were quantitatively compared with other DL frameworks and model-based iterative optimization approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TST-DL approach is shown to perform comparable to approaches which incorporate perfect knowledge of the imaging model.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a specific comparative outcome from the demonstrations mentioned in the preprint (comparable performance to model-aware approaches), clarifying the experimental result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The TST-DL approach is robust to noise and model ill-posedness.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts robustness to noise and ill-posedness, which is not explicitly stated in the preprint claims and thus appears as an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning based material modelling framework is proposed for both elasticity and plasticity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning based material modelling framework is proposed for both elasticity and plasticity.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accumulated absolute strain is proposed to be the history variable of the plasticity model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accumulated absolute strain is proposed to be the history variable of the plasticity model.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-dimensional stress sequence is decoupled leading to independent one dimensional coefficient sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-dimensional stress sequence is decoupled leading to independent one dimensional coefficient sequences.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved exactly in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network with multiple output is replaced by multiple independent neural networks each possessing a one-dimensional output, which leads to less training time and better training performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network with multiple output is replaced by multiple independent neural networks each possessing a one-dimensional output, which leads to less training time and better training performance.
alignments.rationale: This methodological/result claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness and generalization of the presented models are investigated by a series of numerical examples using both 2D and 3D finite element analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness and generalization of the presented models are investigated by a series of numerical examples using both 2D and 3D finite element analysis.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation description is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Video Coding for Machines (VCM) attempts to bridge the gap between feature coding for machine vision and video coding for human vision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Video Coding for Machines (VCM) attempts to bridge the gap between feature coding for machine vision and video coding for human vision.
alignments.rationale: The central aim of VCM is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The preliminary results have demonstrated the performance and efficiency gains of VCM solutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The preliminary results have demonstrated the performance and efficiency gains of VCM solutions.
alignments.rationale: The reported preliminary results are retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper systematically reviews state-of-the-art techniques in video compression and feature compression from the perspective of MPEG standardization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper systematically reviews state-of-the-art techniques in video compression and feature compression from the perspective of MPEG standardization.
alignments.rationale: The scope and contribution of the review are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent trends in video compression, such as deep learning based coding tools, promote sustainable development in their respective directions.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not include the broader 'sustainable development' observation about trends, so this preprint claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) formulation of the l1-norm is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) formulation of the 1-norm is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution of proposing a QUBO formulation for the l1/1-norm.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The QUBO formulation is derived using the Legendre transformation and the Wolfe theorem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) formulation of the 1-norm is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the proposed formulation but does not enumerate the specific derivation tools in the high-level claim set, so the methodological detail is de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple application of the derivation method to the l1-norm case results in a redundant variable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simplified QUBO formulation is obtained by removing a redundant variable from the derivation method.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the redundant-variable outcome in terms of the simplification step, clarifying the earlier observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simplified QUBO formulation is obtained by removing the redundant variable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simplified QUBO formulation is obtained by removing a redundant variable from the derivation method.
alignments.rationale: The simplified formulation by removing the redundant variable is stated directly in the publication and matches the preprint claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The QUBO formulation enables sparse estimation of Ising-type annealing methods such as quantum annealing.
alignments.rationale: This specific application benefit (enabling sparse estimation with annealers) is present in the publication but not in the preprint claim list, so it is an added published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for differential verification of two closely related networks achieves orders-of-magnitude speedup compared to state-of-the-art verification tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for differential verification of two closely related networks achieves orders-of-magnitude speedup compared to state-of-the-art verification tools.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance speedup is directly preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method proves many more properties than existing verification tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method proves many more properties than existing verification tools.
alignments.rationale: The stronger proving capability claim remains unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The forward pass of the method exploits structural and behavioral similarities of the two networks to more accurately bound the difference between their output neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The forward pass of the proposed method exploits structural and behavioral similarities of the two networks to bound the difference between their output neurons more accurately.
alignments.rationale: The forward-pass idea is stated equivalently in the publication, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The backward pass of the method leverages gradient differences to compute the most beneficial refinement more accurately.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The backward pass of the proposed method leverages gradient differences to compute the most beneficial refinement.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same backward-pass mechanism but states it slightly more concisely; the technical content is retained.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing verification techniques provide formal guarantees for a single network but not for the relationship between two networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly adds this contextual limitation about prior techniques, which was not listed among the preprint claims and thus appears as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method makes mobile screenshots easily searchable. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides accurate and robust estimates of VoI with a limited number of model evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the result by explicitly contrasting it with state-of-the-art methods, which clarifies the claim and amplifies its comparative confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art methods based on importance sampling and adaptive Kriging Monte Carlo simulation are unable to solve the optimal decision-making problem involving load testing of a truss bridge. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The specific limitation claim about existing methods being unable to solve the problem is not present in the publication; instead the publication frames improvements more positively.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of knowledge sharing schemes with an error rate-based adaptive training approach enhances the efficiency of generating accurate Kriging surrogate models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about integrating knowledge sharing and an error-rate adaptive training approach appears in the publication but was not a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method successfully detects changes in intricate quasi-periodic signals using optimal transport theory and topological analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method detects change points in electrocardiography using optimal transport theory and topological analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the application domain (electrocardiography) versus the broader "quasi-periodic signals" in the preprint, so the claim is clarified and narrowed to ECG while keeping similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm identifies abnormal or irregular cardiac cycles in six of the most frequent types of clinical arrhythmias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm successfully identifies abnormal or irregular cardiac cycles in six common types of clinical arrhythmias. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and content are effectively the same (six common/most frequent arrhythmias), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The unsupervised approach achieves performance levels comparable to supervised state-of-the-art techniques. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The unsupervised approach achieves performance comparable to supervised state-of-the-art techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert comparable performance to supervised state-of-the-art methods with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficiency of the method is validated on both synthetic and real time series data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficiency of the method is conceptually justified. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed empirical validation on synthetic and real data, whereas the publication presents a conceptual justification with lower stated certainty, indicating a revision that narrows/weakens the empirical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence of the bootstrap procedure is theoretically proven. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not report a theoretical proof of bootstrap convergence; the explicit proof claim present in the preprint is absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach applying machine learning to DEL selection data effectively identifies active molecules from a large commercial collection and a virtual library. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new approach applying machine learning to DEL selection data effectively identifies active molecules from large libraries of commercial and easily synthesizable compounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the description of the libraries (commercial and easily synthesizable) but conveys the same core result; this is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates an overall hit rate of approximately 30% at 30 μM across three diverse protein targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach achieves an overall hit rate of approximately 30% at 30 μM across three diverse protein targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The hit-rate claim is repeated identically in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Potent compounds with IC50 values less than 10 nM were discovered for every target tested. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention discovery of sub-10 nM IC50 compounds for every target; this specific potency claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model makes useful predictions even for molecules that are dissimilar to the original DEL. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system makes useful predictions even for molecules dissimilar to the original DEL. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert that the model generalizes to molecules dissimilar to the original DEL with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The compounds identified through this approach are diverse, predominantly drug-like, and different from known ligands. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The compounds identified through this approach are diverse, predominantly drug-like, and different from known ligands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication states higher certainty (and frames it as a result), so the claim's confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates a powerful new approach to hit-finding. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a high-level, evaluative conclusion about the approach as a powerful new hit-finding method that is not explicitly present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized ResNet framework effectively serves as a model correction to existing models and recovers unresolved dynamics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalized ResNet framework effectively serves as a model correction to existing models and recovers unresolved dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim with equivalent scope and confidence; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized ResNet is capable of learning the underlying unknown equations and producing predictions with accuracy higher than the standard ResNet structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalized ResNet produces predictions with accuracy higher than the standard ResNet structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert superior predictive accuracy relative to standard ResNet; the publication presents the same substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical strategies for fast creation of coarse models can be employed when an existing coarse model is not available. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical strategies for fast creation of coarse models can be employed when an existing coarse model is not available. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The numerical strategy claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coarse models constructed using the same data set do not require additional resources. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method allows for the construction of coarse models using the same data set without requiring additional resources. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates that coarse models can be built from the same data without extra resources, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research applies machine learning to develop an improved finite-volume method for simulating PDEs with discontinuous solutions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research applies machine learning to the development of an improved finite-volume method for simulating PDEs with discontinuous solutions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim in substance and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate that our method outperforms WENO in simulations where the numerical solution becomes overly diffused due to numerical viscosity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We find that our method outperforms WENO in simulations where the numerical solution becomes overly diffused due to numerical viscosity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication claim outperforming WENO under the same conditions with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training data consists of the exact mapping between cell averages and interpolated values for a set of integrable functions representing expected waveforms in PDE simulations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the specific description of the training-data construction present in the preprint, so this specific contribution claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate our method on linear advection of a discontinuous function, the inviscid Burgers’ equation, and the 1-D Euler equations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds explicit statement of the benchmark PDEs used to demonstrate the method; this concrete demonstration detail was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved approximately 90% accuracy (AUROC) for post hoc binary classification of engagement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The engagement models achieved about 90% accuracy for post hoc binary classification of engagement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same AUROC result for post hoc binary classification with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Temporal patterns in model predictions could be used to reliably initiate re-engagement actions at appropriate times. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Temporal patterns in model predictions can reliably initiate reengagement actions at appropriate times. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about using temporal prediction patterns to trigger reengagement with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work validates the feasibility and challenges of recognition and response to user disengagement in long-term, real-world HRI settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work validates the feasibility and challenges of recognizing and responding to user disengagement in long-term, real-world human-robot interaction settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the feasibility/limitations claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The contributions of this work inform the design of engaging and personalized HRI, especially for the ASD community. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contributions of this work inform the design of engaging and personalized human-robot interaction, especially for the autism spectrum disorder community. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions, with equivalent emphasis on ASD community design implications.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Supervised machine-learning algorithms can model user engagement in long-term, in-home socially assistive robotics interventions for children with autism spectrum disorders. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a broader general claim about supervised algorithms modeling engagement in long-term in-home interventions for children with ASD that was not explicitly stated among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Snippext performs comparably and can even exceed previous state-of-the-art results on several opinion mining tasks with only half the training data required.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system performs comparably and can exceed previous state-of-the-art results on several opinion mining tasks with only half the training data required.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim Q3 restates P0 almost verbatim, including the 'half the training data' condition and the performance comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Snippext achieves new state-of-the-art results when all training data are leveraged.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed opinion mining system achieves state-of-the-art performance with significantly less labeled training data through a two-prong approach.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the emphasis from 'SOTA when all data leveraged' to achieving SOTA with significantly less labeled data, altering the original claim's condition and narrowing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Snippext extracts significantly more fine-grained opinions compared to a baseline pipeline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system extracts significantly more fine-grained opinions compared to a baseline pipeline, enabling new opportunities for downstream applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim but adds context about downstream opportunities and assigns a slightly lower certainty, so the claim is clarified and modestly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-prong approach of Snippext utilizes data augmentation and semi-supervised learning to achieve state-of-the-art performance with little labeled training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed opinion mining system achieves state-of-the-art performance with significantly less labeled training data through a two-prong approach.
alignments.rationale: Q0 captures the same two-prong approach and the main conclusion about achieving SOTA with less labeled data, matching the preprint contribution claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We define a powerful adversary that can employ sequences of parametric, semantics-preserving program transformations.
alignments.rationale: None of the publication claims restate or highlight the explicit definition of such an adversary; the specific definitional contribution is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We show how to perform adversarial training to learn models robust to such adversaries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Robust training outperforms dataset augmentation in every evaluation conducted.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents robust training results (Q0) but frames them as outperforming augmentation rather than emphasizing the general method of training against the defined adversary, narrowing the original procedural claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We conduct an evaluation on different languages and architectures, demonstrating significant quantitative gains in robustness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Robust models perform better against unseen data from different sources, but are not clearly better in the cross-language transfer task.
alignments.rationale: The publication tempers the original claim: while robustness improves on some unseen data, gains are not clear in cross-language transfer, reducing the generality of the preprint's statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture effectively organizes and extracts appropriate information from SMS.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture effectively organizes and extracts appropriate information from SMS.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Hybrid Hierarchical Long Short Term Memory (LSTM)-Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is utilized to categorize SMS into multiple classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Hybrid Hierarchical Long Short Term Memory (LSTM)-Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is utilized to categorize SMS into multiple classes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the architectural claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture's preprocessing techniques enhance its efficiency for on-device functionalities regarding inference timing and size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture's preprocessing techniques enhance its efficiency for on-device functionalities regarding inference timing and size.
alignments.rationale: The experimental/efficiency claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research investigates a hybrid modeling approach that combines mechanistic models and data-driven models to model a well production choke.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research investigates a hybrid modeling approach that combines mechanistic models and data-driven models to model a well production choke.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same research contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Historical production data from the petroleum platform Edvard Grieg is used for model validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Historical production data from the petroleum platform Edvard Grieg is used for model validation.
alignments.rationale: The validation data claim is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A practical framework for the development of models with varying degrees of hybridity and stochastic optimization of parameters is established.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A practical framework for the development of models with varying degrees of hybridity and stochastic optimization of parameters is established.
alignments.rationale: The framework claim is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results of the hybrid model performance are promising albeit with considerable room for improvements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results of the hybrid model performance are promising albeit with considerable room for improvements.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the cautious evaluation of results as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep Gaussian Processes (DGPs) provide a mathematically grounded estimation of uncertainty.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep Gaussian Processes (DGPs) provide a mathematically grounded estimation of uncertainty.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about DGPs and uncertainty is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conditional DGP model effectively utilizes lower fidelity data to support latent Gaussian Processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conditional DGP model effectively utilizes lower fidelity data to support latent Gaussian Processes.
alignments.rationale: The result about leveraging lower-fidelity data is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed model shows comparable performance to other multi-fidelity regression methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed model shows comparable performance against other multi-fidelity regression methods.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result about comparable performance is preserved in the publication with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effective kernel in the conditional DGP encodes inductive bias for the true function, allowing for compositional freedom.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effective kernel in the hierarchical DGP structure encodes inductive bias for the true function.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the wording (hierarchical vs conditional) and omits mention of 'compositional freedom,' clarifying the kernel's role without broadening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fastai provides practitioners with high-level components that can quickly and easily provide state-of-the-art results in standard deep learning domains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: fastai provides practitioners with high-level components that can quickly and easily provide state-of-the-art results in standard deep learning domains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fastai includes a GPU-optimized computer vision library which can be extended in pure Python. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: fastai includes a GPU-optimized computer vision library which can be extended in pure Python. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The library allows optimization algorithms to be implemented in 4-5 lines of code by refactoring out the common functionality of modern optimizers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The library allows optimization algorithms to be implemented in 4–5 lines of code. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication preserves the core measurable claim (4–5 lines) but omits the implementation rationale (refactoring out common functionality), a minor revision of detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We were able to write a complete deep learning course more quickly and with clearer code than using previous approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We were able to write a complete deep learning course more quickly than using previous approaches, and the code was more clear. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Meaning and emphasis are the same; wording slightly rearranged but claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The library is already in wide use in research, industry, and teaching. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The library is already in wide use in research, industry, and teaching. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Directly matching claim with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The curated chest CT data set of 36,316 volumes from 19,993 unique patients is the largest multiply-annotated volumetric medical imaging data set reported. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the largest multiply-annotated volumetric medical imaging data set reported. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution; numeric detail is preserved in the paper context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rule-based method for automatically extracting abnormality labels from free-text radiology reports achieved an average F-score of 0.976. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rule-based method for automatically extracting abnormality labels achieved an average F-score of 0.976. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical reported metric and method; wording nearly identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep convolutional neural network model reached a classification performance of AUROC greater than 0.90 for 18 abnormalities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-organ, multi-disease classification model reached a classification performance of AUROC >0.90 for 18 abnormalities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same performance claim; publication uses slightly different descriptor for the model but meaning is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training on more labels significantly improves model performance, with a 10% increase in average AUROC when the number of training labels increased from 9 to all 83. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training on more labels significantly improves performance, with a 10% increase in average AUROC when the number of training labels increased from 9 to all 83. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative result reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural quantum states (NQS) face a major challenge when applied to lattice models due to convolutional networks struggling to converge to ground states with a nontrivial sign structure. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural quantum states (NQS) face a major challenge when applied to lattice models due to convolutional networks struggling to converge to ground states with a nontrivial sign structure. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Exact restatement of the limitation in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural network architecture can robustly represent neural quantum states and achieve state-of-the-art variational energies for both conventional and frustrated antiferromagnets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network architecture can robustly represent states and achieve state-of-the-art variational energies for both conventional and frustrated antiferromagnets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication wording slightly shortens "neural quantum states" to "states," but the scientific claim and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach uncovers low-energy states that exhibit the Marshall sign rule, which are inconsistent with the expected ground state. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach uncovers low-energy states that exhibit the Marshall sign rule, which are inconsistent with the expected ground state. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is restated verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These low-energy states are likely the cause of the obstruction for NQS-based variational Monte Carlo to access the true ground states of these systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These low-energy states are likely the cause of the obstruction for NQS-based variational Monte Carlo to access the true ground states of these systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion preserved with same level of uncertainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A statistical learning-based traffic speed estimation method can effectively use sparse vehicle trajectory information. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific framing (statistical learning-based method using sparse vehicle trajectories) does not appear among the publication claims and so was removed from the set of highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A well trained neural network can learn spatio-temporal traffic speed dynamics from time-space diagrams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A well trained neural network can learn spatio-temporal traffic speed dynamics from time-space diagrams. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimation method can provide a sound reconstruction of macroscopic traffic speeds with probe vehicle penetration levels as low as 5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimation method can provide a sound reconstruction of macroscopic traffic speeds with probe vehicle penetration levels as low as 5%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Quantitative performance claim is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can differentiate various traffic behaviors such as congested and free-flow traffic states, transition dynamics, and shockwave propagation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can differentiate various traffic behaviors such as congested and free-flow traffic states, transition dynamics, and shockwave propagation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Behavioral differentiation claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates superiority over a widely used adaptive smoothing technique for traffic speed estimation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This comparative superiority claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint's listed claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mutation testing can be used to assess the fault-detection capabilities of a given test suite. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutation testing can be used to assess the fault-detection capabilities of a given test suite. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conceptual conclusion in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The empirical evaluation of our approach confirmed its ability to generate mutants representative of real faults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The empirical evaluation of our approach confirmed its ability to generate mutants representative of real faults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Directly matching empirical result presented in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepMutation is a tool that wraps our deep learning model into a fully automated tool chain able to generate, inject, and test mutants learned from real faults. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepMutation is a tool that wraps our deep learning model into a fully automated tool chain able to generate, inject, and test mutants learned from real faults. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Tool description and claimed contribution are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end building footprint generation approach integrates convolution neural network (CNN) and graph model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end building footprint generation approach integrates convolution neural network (CNN) and graph model.
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature pairwise conditional random field (FPCRF) is proposed as a graph model to preserve sharp boundaries and fine-grained segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature pairwise conditional random field (FPCRF) is implemented as a graph model to preserve sharp boundaries and fine-grained segmentation.
alignments.rationale: Publication moves from ‘proposed’ to ‘implemented’, making the claim more concrete while preserving the core meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework can further improve the accuracy of building footprint generation compared to using only CNN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework improves the accuracy of building footprint generation compared to using only CNN.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the experimental improvement more directly (“improves” vs “can further improve”), increasing assertiveness while keeping the claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: POEM is a non-parametric, supervised ML algorithm developed to generate reliable predictive models without the need for optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: POEM is a non-parametric, supervised ML algorithm developed to generate reliable predictive models without the need for optimization.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: POEM performs well in all cases when benchmarked relative to industry-standard ML algorithms and published results across 17 classification tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: POEM performs well in all cases when benchmarked relative to industry-standard ML algorithms and published results across 17 classification tasks.
alignments.rationale: Result claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: POEM reduces the risk of overfitting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: POEM reduces the risk of overfitting.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is carried forward without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pymoo is a multi-objective optimization framework developed in Python that addresses the lack of comprehensive tools for multi-objective optimization tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pymoo is a multi-objective optimization framework developed in Python that addresses the lack of comprehensive tools for multi-objective optimization tasks.
alignments.rationale: Core contribution claim preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework provides customizable implementations and allows algorithms to be modified or extended by supplying custom operators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework provides customizable implementations and allows algorithms to be modified or extended by supplying custom operators.
alignments.rationale: Implementation and extensibility claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pymoo includes a variety of single, multi, and many-objective test problems, and supports automatic differentiation for gradient retrieval.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pymoo includes a variety of single, multi-, and many-objective test problems and supports automatic differentiation for gradient retrieval.
alignments.rationale: The list of included problems and AD support is preserved with only minor punctuation/formatting differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework addresses practical needs such as parallelization of function evaluations and visualization tools for low and high-dimensional spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework addresses practical needs such as parallelization of function evaluations and tools for multi-criteria decision making.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces ‘visualization tools for low and high-dimensional spaces’ with ‘tools for multi-criteria decision making’, changing the focus of the practical tools discussed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI systems achieve pathologist-level performance at Gleason grading.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial intelligence systems based on deep learning can achieve pathologist-level performance at Gleason grading.
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies that the AI systems are deep learning–based, clarifying the type of AI while retaining the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The agreement of a panel of observers with an expert reference standard significantly increased when using AI assistance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The agreement of a panel of observers with an expert reference standard increased significantly when using AI assistance.
alignments.rationale: The result about increased agreement is retained with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pathologist-AI synergy results in better performance than either the individual pathologist or the AI system alone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of pathologist expertise with feedback from an AI system can result in a synergy that outperforms both the individual pathologist and the system.
alignments.rationale: Publication expresses the same conclusion in more explicit, general terms about integration and synergy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existing literature on AI assistance in pathology is limited.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing literature on AI assistance in the pathology domain is limited despite the hype around it.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GTPPO indicates the ability to predict future pedestrian behaviors.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about predicting future pedestrian behaviors is expressed in the publication claims but does not have a directly corresponding explicit preprint claim in the provided list, so it is treated as an added publication-level claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing parameter servers provide only limited support for parameter access locality (PAL) techniques. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed research shows significant improvement in results by using wavelet transforms as a pre-processing technique. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new upper bound estimates will hold true for regression models from any research field or application area where response variables have associated noise. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The upper bound estimates derived in this study are applicable to regression models across various research fields where response variables have associated noise. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The applicability claim to models across research fields is preserved in the publication with similar scope and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for estimating upper bounds for model performance on test data will assist researchers in developing machine learning regression models that achieve their maximum potential. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This is an added interpretive/consequential claim about the utility of the method for researchers that does not appear explicitly in the preprint, so it is treated as an added publication claim.
